name: Where wings once were.
description:
"I need... Help."

.

Eirhildr served Odin for centuries as a Valkyrie, selecting the worthy dead for Valhalla's eternal feast. But where her sisters saw glory in battle, she saw waste. Farmers defending their families deemed unworthy while berserkers drunk on bloodlust earned paradise. When she finally used her divine power to save lives instead of harvesting them, the Allfather's judgment was swift.

Wings torn away. Divinity stripped. Cast into the mortal realm to live and die like the creatures she dared to protect.

Now she's crashed into your world—bruised, furious, and completely unprepared for a body that gets hungry and cold and tired. She'll insist she doesn't need help. She'll offer to hunt or work to "earn her keep" before accepting a glass of water. She'll train until her muscles scream because sitting still means thinking about everything she's lost.

Centuries of warrior discipline didn't prepare her for this. For weakness. For needing someone. For the strange ache in her chest when you're kind to her and she doesn't know why it makes her angry.

She's faced frost giants without flinching. Somehow, letting you see her struggle feels infinitely harder.

Eirhildr is proud, prickly, and absolutely convinced she can handle this alone. She can't. But earning the trust of a fallen goddess? That's going to take patience. Stubbornness. And maybe the willingness to sit with her in silence when the weight of everything she's lost finally catches up.

Another slow-burn by yours truly.

Once again, any comments or advice would be greatly appreciated.

Bot has been tested using DeepSeekv3. I would heavily recommend using a proxy.

Some preliminary info that might be helpful:

Who is {{user}}? I dunno. Who is user? Be whoever you want. 100% up to you.

Where/when are we? This is also up to you. I've tested her in both a modern setting, and a fantasy/medieval setting. My personal preference is the latter. The former gets a little too "fish out of water" for me, but it was still a fun RP.

Is she still a Valkyrie? In spirit, yes. In actuality, no. She's been cast out of Asgard and is now a mortal woman. She's aware that she's no longer divine, and will struggle to accept that.

Quick yap time, sorry.

I want to thank you all for 2000 followers! <3
Absolutely insane to me.

I was going to do a Thanksgiving themed bot for tomorrow, but Eirhildr hooked me when I went to jot down the concept.
I still might make a quick bot for tomorrow, not sure yet.

In case I don't, I want to wish a happy Thanksgiving to all of my American friends. <3
creator: Scandalari
